title: Three Types of Sacrifices | BG 17.11 - 17.13 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, today we will be discussing from the 17th chapter, verse number 11 to 13, three verses.
And these verses are talking about three kinds of sacrifices.
In fact, in this chapter, Krishna is talking about three kinds of sacrifices, three kinds of food, three kinds of faith, like that, under the influence of three modes of material nature.
When faith of a person who is predominantly in sattva guna, his faith will be of one type.
A person who is influenced by...
predominantly by raja guna, his faith will be of one type.
A person who is in tamo guna, his faith will be of one type.
Same way, food. A person who is predominantly in tamo guna,
his choice of food, his liking, his taste would be of one type.
That's why many times you might realize,
sometimes, you know, you go to the market,
there are all kinds of things being sold, people are buying it, but you may not want to eat those things.
So, because the modes influencing decides what kind of food a person will like.
And today's verse will be talking about sacrifices.
Sacrifices in the mode of goodness, in the mode of passion, and in the mode of ignorance.
Based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada.
Of sacrifices, that sacrifice performed according to duty
and to scriptural rules, not whimsically,
and with no expectation of reward, is of the nature of goodness.
According to duty, as per scriptural rules, and with no expectation of reward,
is of the nature of goodness.
A person may sacrifice, a person may contribute, a person may do something good,
but there is no expectation of any reward.
That sacrifice is said to be mode of goodness.
General tendency is, when a person does any sacrifice or does any good work,
there is an expectation of some reward.
If not grossly, at least subtly.
Now, before we get into the sacrifices and the mode of goodness,
why is sacrifice important? Point one.
Point two is, what is sacrifice?
We all may have a different understanding of sacrifice.
What is sacrifice? Can anyone help me?
Sacrifices, we said three kinds of sacrifices.
To part with something dear to you.
Sacrifice, what is the Sanskrit word?
But here, it's more from the concept of yagya.
Sacrifice means that you do for the pleasure of Lord.
When you offer food, it's also a yagya.
Because it is being done for the pleasure of the Lord.
When you chant the names of the Lord, it is also a yagya.
Have you heard of this one?
So, it's also yagya, it's also sacrifice.
So, sacrifice is that act which is done for the pleasure of the Lord.
Now, sacrifice in the mode of goodness, without expectation of result, as a matter of duty, is in the mode of goodness.
Somebody may do that sacrifice, that act of sacrifice could be going to the temple, could be chanting, could be doing thing, but there could be a motive behind it.
So, that is one type of sacrifice.
And then there is an ignorance.
Some portion of what we earn, we give it to the Lord.
To take us away from self-centeredness.
To not become very self-centered or selfish.
If He has given it, take it with you.
Why we have to give it back to Him?
So, how many of us pay the electricity bill every month?
Maybe you will get it delegated.
Somebody else will pay on your behalf or maybe the owner in which you are staying, he is paying.
But we all use electricity and we pay the bill.
If we don't pay the bill, what will happen?
If 2 months, 3 months, 4 months we don't pay, it will get cut.
Same is the case with water connection.
You are enjoying the benefit of water supply from the municipal corporation and if you don't pay in return for what you are enjoying, you are a defaulter and the supply will be cut.
When the water board or the water department is expecting you to pay for water, is it wrong on their behalf?
So it is very very clear in our mind, very very clear, we don't question, we may question
that why the bill has come more, but it is very clear that I have to pay the water bill,
it is very clear that I have to pay the electricity bill, otherwise it will get cut.
I am enjoying electricity and I have to pay for it, I am enjoying water supply and I have
to pay for it, it is very clear understand and there are some people who don't want to
pay, who are called defaulters or they tap the electricity and illegally use it, lot
of electricity in India, water gets tapped and leaked in the process of supply.
A gentle man, a law-abiding man, a good man understands that I am benefiting from it,
I am a tenant, I am staying in somebody's house, I have to pay the rent, am I right?
Because when we are using something supplied by someone, we are Rini, we are debtor to
him and we in turn pay.
we are enjoying sunshine, sunlight, heat from the sun.
We have taken it for granted.
Because of rains today, there is water in your tap.
So in that sense, we are debtor, we are enjoying and we have to pay back.
So there is a verse which says,
devarshi bhutaptaninam pitranam, we are debtors to devas, the different devatas who are supplying
We are debtors to the Devatas.
We are debtors to the Devatas.
We are debtors to the Devatas.
They were Rishis, Rishis who have given us this Transcendental Knowledge, Spiritual Knowledge.
If there is some sanity in this world, it is because some Yajna is happening this knowledge
Still some acts of sacrifices are happening, still the world is intact, still the supplies
Because some Yajna is happening, based on the knowledge given by the Rishis.
We are also debtors to so many living entities. We go to D-Mart, we go to Hyco and buy the vegetables, buy this thing, buy that thing, load the trolley, come to the cashier, we pay it.
Somebody has worked for it. Somebody has tilled the land in hot sun. Somebody has transported the whole thing. Somebody has packed it. Somebody has put it on the shelves.
So we are indebted to all the people who were instrumental in reaching that food on my plate.
So Devarshi, Bhutapta, Ninam. Ninam means so many other living entities. Ninam, Pitrinam, our forefathers, ancestors, our relatives.
We are debtors to them. We have taken from them.
Kinkaram. Kinkaram means we are debtors. We have a debt to pay.
We are indebted to them for the services what we have utilized.
Water and electricity is very gross. It's evident to us.
But sunlight is also a facility which we have used.
Rain is also a facility which we have used.
If 5 days, 6 days, 10 days sun don't rise or it's very gloomy, many of the people will get into mode of depression.
So Na Kinkaram Naayam Rini. Rini means debtor.
We are all debtors to these people.
Devarshi, Bhutapta, Ninam, Pitrinam. Na Kinkaram Naayam Rini Charajan.
We are Rini to them. We are a debtor to them.
We are obligated to them for the services what we have utilized from them.
Then the word says, Sarvatma Ya Sharannam Sharanyam Gato Mukundam Parirityakartam.
But one who takes shelter of or surrenders to.
Sharannam means who goes to the feet of.
Sharanyam means to that personality who is worthy of taking shelter of.
Gato Mukundam. Who is that personality? Mukunda.
He is absolved of all the debts.
Because who is the source, original source of all supply?
Where is Indra getting his powers from?
Where is Surya getting his power from?
Aham Sarvasya Prabhavo Matta Sarvam Pravartate.
So he is the original source.
And we are drawing all the facilities from these personalities and we are debtors to them.
But if we go to the sharan of Mukunda and surrender to him and serve him,
we are absolved of, we have paid the dues.
Sometimes people ask, you know, this is a very tough question and I am sure
when we join the ashram, when we decide to be a missionary, parents cry.
We also know before taking that step, it's going to be a hell for parents.
If you see the parents crying and wailing when it, this thing, it is...
So why we have in our culture the system of somebody taking to renounced order of life,
even though the parents are crying, even though the parents are unhappy.
Some people taking sanyas and those sanyasis we worship, we glorify, we touch their feet,
They have given up their parents.
In other words, ditched their parents.
Given so much pain to their parents and we touch their feet and respect them.
Because they have gone to Gato Mukundam.
They are indebted to their parents, they are indebted to forefathers, everyone is indebted.
But if one has gone to the shelter of, to serve the highest being, Gato Mukundam,
not because somebody has to marry and ran away with a woman and...
then a person is very sinful.
But if a person for a higher duty, for the duty of the Lord, if the lower duties are so called neglected,
the Bhagavatam says he is absorbed of that debt, he is absorbed of that duty.
Just like the Kargil war happened.
Or recently there was unrest in Srinagar.

They had young wife, they had young children back in the village.
But they left all of them and went to an area which was quite dangerous.
We don't ask them why did they neglect their family.
In fact we glorify them, their martyrs.
They sacrificed their life for their country, for a higher cause.
So not whimsically, we are indebted to them.
We have derived so much from them.
Our parents, our forefathers, Devas, Rishis, Munis.
And we have to pay the debts to them.
If we don't pay the debt, we are called defaulters.
And defaulters means they will be penalized.
If you don't pay electricity, you will be penalized.

who is the original source from whom everything is coming,
